D6 Display L1 Remember Atom

The Subpixel Loupe

zoom into the screen until each pixel splits into its three coloured lamps — red, green, blue

Every colour on a screen is a lie of proximity: each pixel is really three tiny lamps — a red, a green, and a blue stripe — too small to resolve. White is all three at full; yellow is red and green lit with blue dark. Magnify enough and the illusion comes apart into stripes.

Raise the zoom. At life size the colour bars look solid; enlarged, each resolves into its lit and dark subpixel stripes. Notice white needs all three, while pure red leaves two stripes black.
